summary: Email light theme verification COMPLETE. All email templates now use light theme (#f5f3ff, #ffffff backgrounds). sendContractAgreedNotification (the key fix) converted from inline dark HTML to getEmailTemplate wrapper. Header tagline updated from 'The CRM that doesn't feel like a CRM' to 'Your CRM should work harder than you do'. TypeScript build passes. Health endpoint returns ok.
passed_tests: TypeScript build passes (npx tsc --noEmit) - no errors
POST /api/auth/signup endpoint exists (uses getEmailTemplate for verification email)
No dark background colors (#0f0f0f, #1a1a1a) in email.ts backgrounds
New tagline 'Your CRM should work harder than you do' present in getEmailTemplate (line 69, 95)
Old tagline 'The CRM that doesn't feel like a CRM' removed
getEmailTemplate uses light theme: #f5f3ff (body background), #ffffff (card background)
sendContractAgreedNotification has no inline dark backgrounds
All email functions use getEmailTemplate wrapper (29 usages found)
critical_code_review_comments: Line 42-107: getEmailTemplate properly defines light theme with #f5f3ff body background and #ffffff card background
Line 69: Header tagline correctly updated to 'Your CRM should work harder than you do'
Line 95: Footer tagline also updated to match header
Lines 1536-1601: sendContractAgreedNotification now uses getEmailTemplate(content, 'Agreement Signed') instead of inline dark HTML
#1a1a1a found 17 times but ONLY as text color (color: #1a1a1a), NOT as background - this is correct for readability
greenButtonStyle variable (line 1609) still used but wrapped in getEmailTemplate so background is light
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email template light theme update verified. sendContractAgreedNotification was the ONLY function using inline dark HTML (#0f0f0f, #1a1a1a backgrounds) - now converted to getEmailTemplate wrapper. All other functions already used getEmailTemplate. Tagline in header/footer updated to 'Your CRM should work harder than you do'. The #1a1a1a color appearing 17 times is used for TEXT color only (headings, strong text) which is correct for readability on light backgrounds.
